Herkesin kendi diliyle ve inancıyla bu ülkede eşit bir şekilde yaşadığı ve bu eşitliğin
demokratik bir anayasayla garanti altına alındığı bir Türkiye elbette bu sorunları bu şekilde aşabilir. Demokratik bir cumhuriyeti hep beraber inşa edebiliriz. Demokratik cumhuriyeti inşa etmemiz için 85 milyon yurttaşımızı kucaklayan bir politika yürütmek zorundayız; cesur olmak zorundayız, sorunlarımızla yüzleşmek zorundayız, aksi takdirde bunları başarmak mümkün değildir. Bunun için de radikal bir demokrasi mücadelesi vermek zorundayız, aksi takdirde başaramayız.
Değerli halklarımız, değerli işçi kardeşlerim, ekranları başında bizleri izleyen değerli yurttaşlarımız; bizler bu sorun yumağına rağmen asla enseyi karartmayacağız. Baskılara rağmen bu toplum bu gidişata, bu sisteme, bu rejime rızalık vermiyor, vermeyecek. Fabrikalardan, alanlardan, sokaklardan yoksulların, işçilerin, emekçilerin, kadınların, doğa ve insan hakları savunucularının seslerini hep beraber duyuyoruz ve biz, onlarla birlikte aktif olarak mücadele yürütüyoruz. Bizler Türkiye halkları olarak köklerimizden aldığımız gelenekle, bilinçle, mücadele kültürüyle ve birikimiyle yolumuza devam edeceğiz. Umut her gün büyüyorsa mücadelemize olan sarsılmaz güvenimizdendir. Bize dayatılan değil hak ettiğimiz özgür ve onurlu bir yaşamı hep birlikte kuracağız. Bunun sözünü burada bütün Türkiye halklarına, 85 milyon yurttaşımıza veriyoruz; bizler mücadeleyle kazanacağız. Bizler "77" 1 Mayıslarında özne olmuş bir toplumuz, 15-16 Haziran işçi direnişlerinde başarmış bir toplumuz;
TEKEL direnişlerinde, barış mücadelelerinde, kadın mücadelelerinde başarmışız. Bu köklerimizi ve bu birikimimizi asla unutmadan demokratik bir Türkiye, demokratik bir cumhuriyet için hep birlikte çalışmaya devam edeceğiz.
Genel Kurulu saygıyla selamlıyorum. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar)
records.englishTranslation:
TULAY HATIMOĞULLARI ORUÇ (Adana) ON BEHALF OF DEM PARTY GROUP - Mr. President, esteemed members of parliament and our valued people watching us on the screen; I greet you all with love and respect.
I would like to send my greetings from here to our dear friends who are in prison because of their thoughts, their political work, and their free use of their pens. (Applause from DEM Party benches)
Again, my greetings to the Peace Mothers, who are the bright face of this country and who do not hesitate even one step from saying "peace" despite the pain they endured.
I'm sending. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
And my greetings to the dear women who are exposed to violence every day but who, despite everything, do not hesitate to fight for their rights; Greetings a thousand times. (Applause from DEM PARTY rows)
Dear people, unfortunately, people, the poor, workers and laborers all over the world are paying the price of the crisis of capitalism, which does not offer a future for humanity. Unable to overcome its own crisis, capitalism is trying to overcome this crisis with state violence and they are trying to silence people. They are driven out of their minds and afraid of the opposition because they know that they are in an unsustainable situation under normal circumstances; as they feared here, in this hall. Either workers want their rights; What if the workers and the poor organize themselves? What if the unions decide to strike; Women and young people in this country say, "Let's have democracy and be fed." If he takes to the streets saying "this government loses its mind; In Türkiye, global capital and the entire state that protects it are losing their minds, and they are afraid of this. They are afraid of Kurds' and Alevis' demand for equal citizenship, and for this reason, the rulers are very uneasy in Türkiye, as everywhere else, and use all kinds of tyranny to prevent a possible situation.
One of the countries that best implemented the neoliberal policies of capitalism was the AKP in Türkiye. He designed Türkiye according to the needs of global capital
and he literally threw his citizen into the lion's mouth. For this reason, imperialist powers should give medals to the AKP government. It is this government that has made the most privatizations in Türkiye, sold the most public goods, and privatized public economic enterprises; He did not step back from selling to local and foreign capital and then came out and said "We are local and national." They put forward lies such as: Erdogan: "I will run this country like a company." he said; He really ran it like a company, but he also ruined the company. "We are hungry" to cover up the bankruptcy. To people who say, "We are poor." They labeled people who said this as "terrorists" and started putting them in prison.
Dear citizens, as I mentioned at the beginning of my speech, humanity and our world are on the verge of an era transformation and now we are preparing for a new transformation. With the developed artificial intelligence and 5G technologies, we are going through a process of revolutions in the technology and digital world that will open the doors of a brand new civilization that will change the entire future of the world.

If they were to use this development of technology to quench the thirst in the world, to eliminate hunger, to eliminate poverty, to establish peace and tranquility, great humanity would gain in the world, but unfortunately they do not use it like this and unfortunately they use artificial intelligence and 5G technology to destroy humanity in this country and all over the world, they use it to produce weapons and they use it to disrupt the relations and forms of production.
Our dear people,
Global capital - as I stated at the beginning - does not impose its global crisis only in the form of austerity on citizens; At the same time, it is trying to get out of this crisis by provoking wars and conflicts on a world scale. If we look back at our recent history, the bombing of the Twin Towers on September 11, the subsequent invasion of Afghanistan, and the interventions in the Arab Spring, which started in Tunisia in 2010 and spread to the Middle East and North Africa. Imperialist powers turned the Arab Spring into the Arab winter. War is everywhere; When we look from Russia and Ukraine, we see that the war has spread to the West. When we look at the China-Taiwan equation, we see that the war has spread to other regions, but no matter how the war spreads under these conditions, ultimately, we still see the regional form of the imperialist powers' sharing wars and third world sharing wars in the Middle East and African countries.
It is clear that when we look at the developments in Syria, the region is being redesigned. The ultimate goals of the ongoing wars in the Middle East are obvious: to neutralize Iran and draw it into war if they can, to limit Russia, to block China's Belt and Road Project, and to prevent global capital from emerging as a rising threat from another bloc; As a result of all this, blocking the Asia-Pacific line and, if necessary, escalating wars in this sense; That's their whole goal. We cannot handle the developments in Syria independently of these.
From here, I would like to remind the Turkish Grand National Assembly that:
I would like to underline this thickly so that we can all understand more why Türkiye needs internal peace: Although it seems that it has not been brought to the agenda sufficiently yet, the dynamics of the tension that will reach the Red Sea from the northern line, and the Eastern Mediterranean and Cyprus further south are being laid.
And in such a process, we cannot consider Israel's intervention in Gaza independently of this. While Palestinians are being massacred in Gaza today, unfortunately the whole world, especially Türkiye and the current government in Türkiye, has failed. While people and children were being massacred in Gaza, you could not take a step that could stop these attacks, and the whole world could not.
We are faced with an endless, unsolved problem caused by the "nation state" approach. Nationalism, political Islam, sectarianism and male-dominated ideology have displaced millions from their homes. Look, the conflicts that have been going on in the Middle East and North Africa for a long time as a result of the interventions of imperialist powers directly affect Türkiye, our home and our doorstep.

We need to state that these developments in Syria underline the environment and conditions of a larger war, including Iran, and special studies are being carried out on this. We must evaluate the latest developments in Syria without forgetting these plans for a moment, and we must evaluate them from a place that knows that the doors to a bigger war will open. Al-Qaeda, Al-Nusra, which are the products of imperialist powers, and later ISIS and HTS, which are the extensions of Al-Nusra in Syria.
We know very well that many large and small organizations emerged from here, including, and these organizations have now taken the stage once again.
This gang organization, which was produced by the government in Türkiye, was previously known as the "Free Syrian Army", and was later renamed as the "Syrian National Army", and was trained and equipped in Türkiye, is currently operating in Syria again. The Syrian National Army is waging a proxy war in Syria. Who is he attacking now? It is used against the Kurds and Syrian people in Northeast Syria. The greatest evil this government has done to Türkiye and the region is; To continue the deadlock on the Kurdish issue, to be carried away by Neo-Ottomanist dreams and to pursue a foreign policy that pursues these, and to cooperate with and support Salafi jihadist organizations for this purpose.
And if we look at what kind of a picture all of these returned to Türkiye, our dear people, look, what was their feedback to our country? It has brought Türkiye into conflict with almost all the countries in the region. In Türkiye, the Ankara Gar massacre, Suruç and Reyhanlı massacres, Antep wedding attack, massacre and many more massacres that I cannot list here were carried out by these organizations that they trained, equipped, supported and opened the border to. In other words, those trained and equipped by this government became the murderers of Türkiye's citizens.
LEYLA ŞAHİN MASTER (Ankara) - What else!
TULAY HATIMOĞULLARI ORUÇ (Continued) - They burned the Turkish Flag at the Reyhanlı border because you cut their salaries and did not raise them, and because you once offered to meet with Assad.
You couldn't raise your voice.
When we look at the policies you follow under the name of "security", when we compare the day the Syrian war started in 2011 with today, Türkiye's borders have never been this insecure since the establishment of Türkiye until today. Kurds living in northern and eastern Syria are democratic, have adopted a culture of co-existence with different peoples, and have a libertarian approach to women; They are also secular. We have repeatedly said from this podium: Should the Kurdish people with these characteristics be our neighbors, or should ISIS and similar organizations be our neighbors?
ASUMAN ERDOĞAN (Ankara) - There is no such thing. Are you defending Assad?
TULAY HATIMOĞULLARI ORUÇ (Continued) - I would also like to remind you this: Those who came to this podium just described the people living in the lands there in northern and eastern Syria, publicly known as "Rojava", in such a way that it is as if there are no citizens, no civilians there, everyone is waiting with guns in their hands. We are talking about big cities, cities where millions of people live.

In the heart of Ankara, just as you send your children to school, go to the hospital, and go to work every morning, such civilians live there. Therefore, it is necessary to know and understand that geography well.
LEYLA ŞAHİN MASTER (Ankara) - People were in prisons.
TULAY HATIMOĞULLARI ORUÇ (Continued) - Our attitude towards Syria, in the grip of genocide in the Middle East, is very clear. The ongoing interventions of gangs in some regions must end immediately and the guns must be silenced. The Syrian people must determine the fate of Syria on a democratic and peaceful basis, contribute to the process of making a democratic constitution there, and foreign interventions must end immediately. this
Everyone gave the message here, yes; Everyone talked about a democratic Syria at this podium, but those who talked about a democratic Syria at this podium, that is, the government and its partners, said this: "Democratic Syria." But what did he do next? According to the news we received today, 12 civilians, most of whom were children and women, were killed by Turkish Armed Forces' UCAVs in Ayn Issa.
LEYLA ŞAHİN USTA (Ankara) - You cannot say such a thing to the Turkish Armed Forces.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - Ms. Leyla, will you listen; Our Chairman is speaking, you are the Deputy Chairman of the Group, what a shame!
TULAY HATIMOĞULLARI ORUÇ (Continued) - Manbij has been bombed for days and places where civilians live are being bombed. We will say civil peace in Türkiye, we will say peace, but we will hit Manbij, Ayn Isa, Tel Rifat; There is no such world, it cannot exist. This is our call from here: Turkish Armed Forces must put an end to these attacks carried out there.
Arabs, Kurds, Armenians, Turkmens, Druzes, Arab Alevis, Nusayris, Ismailis, Assyrians and all the different peoples and beliefs that I cannot list here have a culture of coexistence, and we must all contribute to the formation of a common, inclusive democratic constitution in which everyone is represented. The status of the Kurds must be recognized absolutely. If we say democratic Syria, democratic Middle East, we will not be afraid or shy of our Kurdish brothers there; We must find ways to live together with our Kurdish brothers there, and this Parliament must work for this.
Our dear people, dear MPs; unfortunately, in dizzying structural transformations
Türkiye has become a country that has lost its course in foreign policy. We are going through a period where foreign politics is closely related to domestic politics and affects each other intensely. This is the case not only for our country but for all countries of the world. Türkiye's foreign policy is currently not rational and is stuck between the West and Eurasia. They pursued a pendulum policy, "How can we take advantage of the cracks?" They tried to say this, but the situation Türkiye has reached is obvious. We must be able to evaluate regional risks in general terms. Those who govern this country must make their choices on the side of the people. Cooperation with anti-democratic forces and inhumane forces must be stopped. It is clear that one cannot proceed with the heroic politics attached to the Neo-Ottoman ideology fed by rotten ideas in the garbage dump of history, and one must be at peace with this truth.

The gains of the Kurdish people in the Middle East are also the gains of the Turkish people, let's not forget this even for a moment. The alliance of Kurds and Turks on democratic grounds will be an important model for the entire Middle East. We have said this many times that a Türkiye that has solved the Kurdish problem internally can undertake a peace role in the Middle East, that this can be sincere, and that it can show such a model.
The earth, and especially our geography, is really tired of war and blood. That's why I want to call out to the whole world from here: Look, the world is facing the threat of nuclear weapons, the world is on the verge of the third great world war;
At such a time, the voice of peace must rise much louder and bolder. The formation of a peace movement by anti-war forces all over the world and the formation of an internationalist peace movement is essential for all the people of the world more than anything else, and this needs to be formed urgently.
In this situation where chaos is deepening in the world and the region, Türkiye needs to do its part in this regard, dear friends, and today we are talking about the budget; While talking about the budget, we also have to talk about the cost of war and conflicts and special war policies. When we look at the expenditures focused solely on the Kurdish issue in the war and conflicts that have been going on for more than forty years, more than 3 trillion dollars have been spent. What was this money spent on? When we think that such a large budget has been spent on bullets, tanks, artillery, UAVs and SIHAs and that such a large budget is used for the people, workers and laborers, believe me, our Turkish worker brothers in Konya and Trabzon will also be fed; Diyarbakır, Batman, Şırnak, Ağrı, Muş and Siirt will not be among the poorest in the economic index.
Dear friends, without wasting any time, both politics, the Parliament and the society need to assume a great duty and responsibility on this issue. As DEM PARTY, we declare once again that we will work without any sacrifice to ensure an honorable peace both inside and outside. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
Dear MPs, our citizens who watch us on their screens; budget cold numbers
It is entirely about planning political choices, not the product. We have said at every opportunity that we do not have millions of citizens in the budget of the People's Alliance; There are no workers, labourers, poor people, tradesmen, farmers, women, children or disabled people. They see nature as existing to plunder, and from here I salute the ongoing resistance in the Kaz Mountains, wish them success, and say that we will all fight together. A handful of supporters have profit, the gang of 5 has gain. The country was sold parcel by parcel, leaving nothing that belonged to the public.
When the lightning struck, inflation was 38.21 percent; now "Fallen." When we look at the inflation figures they say, according to TÜİK data, where the figures were prepared in the palace, the inflation is 47.09, but the real inflation is 80 percent. Interest was 8.5 percent, now it is 50 percent; This is one of the highest interest rates in the world. The dollar exchange rate was 21.14, now it is 35 TL despite all the pressures.

Dear people of Türkiye, look, this government has divided Türkiye into two in this sense, in this sense we mentioned; On one side is the poor majority, that is, 81 percent of this society, and on the other side is the rich minority. This is the greatest betrayal of Beytullem and this betrayal will go down in history in this way.
Our dear people, dear MPs; Throughout the summer, we, as DEM PARTY, held "Bread and Justice" meetings. At the same time, together with our Women's Council
"We insist on an equal and free life, we are against the deepening women's poverty." We continued the campaign and went to many different parts of Türkiye, east, west, north and south; We met with workers, laborers, the poor, we met with women domestic workers; Just touch, hear a thousand sighs! To briefly summarize the scene we saw, it was as follows: We saw women waiting at the door of the vegetable market to take rotten vegetables and fruits home as food; We saw seasonal agricultural workers working ten hours a day and receiving a daily wage of 600 TL; We saw workers and laborers working without security in under-the-counter workshops to make ends meet; We have seen women working at home who have no guarantee or security, both material and moral; We have seen producers who cannot afford the cost of the tea they collect in the Black Sea, the peanuts they collect in Urfa and Antep, the tomatoes and peppers planted in Antalya and Manisa; We have seen the experiences of the workers working in the construction sector, which made the gang of 5, from which the AKP gave away almost the entire existence of the country, as rich as Karun and gave the figures of hormonal economic growth; We saw how the childhood and labor of children who were used as cheap labor for capital through MESEM were stolen; In a place where even hay is imported, we saw that animal husbandry was on the verge of being finished; We saw earthquake victims. While the Vice President was making his presentation here just now, he said, "One of the main reasons why we have a budget deficit is the earthquake." he said. It was a pandemic during the pandemic, God knows what it was before the pandemic, I don't remember what you said.
And look, the earthquake victims are still in the same place - almost two years later - there is still no proper step taken, except for a few regions, and there is no solution offered by the government. Especially in Hatay, which is always treated as a third person by the state, there are still houses waiting to be demolished and people live in containers. Reserve area discussions, other discussions and now the end of the force majeure in the earthquake zone on November 30. Here we reiterate our call once again: In order to heal the wounds of the earthquake, the largest share in this budget should be allocated for this purpose; The budget does not have a deficit because you allocate this, the budget has a deficit because you forgive the taxes of the rich; I would like to remind you of this here.
Look, TÜRK-İŞ determined the hunger line as 20,561.65 TL and the poverty line as 66,976.07 TL in its 2024 November report. The minimum wage has been increased once in 2024, but those who refrain from increasing the minimum wage act without seeing this hunger and poverty line and without considering the situation of the citizens. Here, 50 million of our citizens live below the hunger and poverty line, and this is a very difficult process.

Let us know well that there are no retirees in this budget, we do not have an old uncle who fell off the roof in Konya and died because he was retired and could not make ends meet in this budget, there are no university students, there are no motocourier workers, we do not have the 301 workers we lost in Soma, we do not have the 9 mine workers we lost in İliç; brutally murdered,
no abused children; There are no Delicates or Smurfs in this budget; There is no woman who left her children in the shack because she had to work and lost her 5 children to fire, and there are no children who burned to death. Disabled people are not in this budget, women whose efforts are not seen are not in this budget; no, no, no! There is really no public in this budget; There are interests of capital in this budget, there are interests of a handful of rich classes in this budget, and this is the story of the 2025 budget.
Dear women, we are the ones who are not in this budget the most. We, as DEM PARTY, fought at every opportunity to make this budget a gender-sensitive budget. Women are subjected to violence and murdered every day; Ayşenur Halil, İkbal Uzuner; Women were murdered in a murderous and brutal manner using ISIS-like methods. There is no limit to violence against women in Türkiye, but women are not included in this budget. There are no deterrent laws regarding violence against women, and the male judiciary, which does have laws, is used in favor of men. You withdrew from the Istanbul Convention and opened Law No. 6284 to discussion. You brought up the right to alimony, one of the most important achievements of women, for discussion. There is no shelter, it is not even on the agenda and we say that this budget can never be a women's budget.
We attach great importance to the active participation of women in the social and public sphere. For this reason, as DEM PARTY, we attach importance to co-presidency and equal representation. Especially in our municipalities,
In our municipalities, there are women's directorates, women's departments, women's support houses, shelters and very important activities that I cannot list here, there are very important studies on women. But do you know what this government is doing? What are they doing to erase the name of women from the municipality and ignore our co-chairmanship system? They appoint trustees to eliminate the will of the people, the will of the Kurds, the will of the elected, and the right to vote and be elected. However, what needs to happen is to support these women's institutions, and the first job of the appointed trustee was to close down the women's institutions in our municipalities.
Dear women, we will not make our necks dark; Of course, the days will come when we will make our own budget, and we will all see the days when we will establish our women's ministry. The most important activity of our Ministry will be the effective fight against violence against women. We will establish accessible nursery and care centers in the native language. Despite the pressures we experience, we women will continue to fight for our bodies, our labor and our identity. Even if you ban it, we will continue to say "."(*). (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
(Microphone turned off)
TULAY HATIMOĞULLARI ORUÇ (Continued) - Yes, what should be in this budget or what should be in a budget that is on the side of the people? We must act with the motto of budget for bread and justice. We must provide basic income to households with no income or below a certain income for a life worthy of human dignity.

We must provide electricity, water, natural gas and internet as free public services up to the limit of need. Staple food as an emergency response
In this section, non-Turkish words were expressed by the orator.
VAT on products should be abolished and the prices of basic foods on the shelves should be fixed. The tax has put a strain on workers, tradesmen and small-scale businesses. Shopkeepers are closing their shutters, large capital owners are evading taxes; Therefore there should be a wealth tax; A system should be built in which less taxes are collected from less and more taxes are collected from more.
We must provide rental support to poor families. We must appoint teachers who have not been appointed. We must reinstate those who are subject to decree to their duties by paying their material and moral compensation. The minimum wage should be 35 thousand TL for now, as we say as the DEM PARTY, but we know that this decreases against inflation, so we especially underline that it should be updated every three months according to the rising inflation rate.
Agriculture has been completed in Türkiye. With the agricultural policies it has implemented since the AKP came to power in 2002, Türkiye has turned from an exporting country to an importing country. We import meat, we import wheat and yarn. Should Türkiye have come to this situation? But unfortunately you brought it. In Türkiye, which is one of the most important agricultural countries in the world with its soil, air, water, Çukurova, Harran Plain, Konya Plain and pastures, we promise that when we come to rule this country, we will definitely come to a place that exceeds our previous position with a radical agricultural policy. “There are no resources for this.” Don't say. By ensuring justice in taxes, by expropriating, by giving importance to real production instead of imaginary economic growth, by preventing war, conflict,
By giving up allocating money for special war policies, stopping guarantee payments to public-private cooperation projects, closing the Turkey Wealth Fund - which works like the parallel budget of this government - by including extra-budgetary funds into the budget, eliminating the covert appropriation and many other solutions that I cannot list here, of course, the resources of this country, the source of this budget, are created and we must create them. It is possible to budget for bread and justice; As long as we focus on people, nature, peace and justice when making a budget, it is possible to do this, but it is not possible to do this with the Presidential government system. Therefore, this system needs to change radically. "With this system, there will be rule of law." You said, you created the law of superiors. "We will get rid of the military tutelage regime." You said, the tutelage of the palace is now over all institutions. For this reason, we say that it is of course possible to overcome the multiple crises that Türkiye is in, that is, the economic, social and political crisis, but radical, fundamental changes are needed for this. This system cannot go any further in its current state. The way out of these multiple crises is possible with a democratic mind; It is possible through pluralism, not monism; It is possible by building a democratic Türkiye. Without expressing the different peoples and beliefs living in Türkiye as "of this origin" or "of that origin".

Just as we do not say "of Turkish origin", everyone is an equal citizen of this country; Turks, Kurds, Laz, Arabs and Circassians. Everyone lives equally in this country with their own language and belief and this equality is
A Türkiye guaranteed by a democratic constitution can of course overcome these problems in this way. We can build a democratic republic together. In order to build the democratic republic, we have to implement a policy that embraces our 85 million citizens; We have to be brave, we have to face our problems, otherwise it is not possible to achieve them. To achieve this, we have to wage a radical democracy struggle, otherwise we cannot succeed.
Our dear people, our dear worker brothers, our dear citizens who watch us in front of their screens; Despite this tangle of problems, we will never give up. Despite the pressure, this society does not and will not consent to this course, this system, this regime. We all hear the voices of the poor, workers, laborers, women, nature and human rights defenders from factories, fields and streets, and we actively struggle together with them. We, as the people of Türkiye, will continue on our way with the tradition, consciousness, culture of struggle and knowledge we inherit from our roots. If hope grows every day, it is because of our unwavering confidence in our struggle. Together, we will build a free and honorable life that we deserve, not one that is imposed on us. We promise this here to all the people of Türkiye, our 85 million citizens; We will win through struggle. We are a society that was the subject of the May 1st of "77", a society that succeeded in the workers' resistance of June 15-16;
We have succeeded in TEKEL resistance, peace struggles and women's struggles. We will continue to work together for a democratic Türkiye, a democratic republic, without ever forgetting our roots and this knowledge.
I respectfully greet the General Assembly. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)

records.originalTurkish:
DEM PARTİ GRUBU ADINA TULAY HATIMOĞULLARI ORUÇ (Adana) - Sayın Başkan, değerli milletvekilleri ve ekranı başında bizleri izleyen değerli Türkiye halkları; hepinizi sevgiyle, saygıyla selamlıyorum ve sözlerime başlarken, haksız ve hukuksuz şekilde cezaevinde tutulan sevgili Figen Yüksekdağ, Selahattin Demirtaş, Leyla Güven ve Ayşe Gökkan şahsında bütün siyasi tutuklu arkadaşlarımızı buradan sevgiyle selamlıyorum. (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar)
Değerli arkadaşlar, 2026 bütçesinin Komisyon aşaması bitti, Genel Kurulda yoğun bir mesai olacak, Komisyonda da çok yoğun bir mesai oldu. Ben partim adına bütçe sürecinde gerek Komisyon aşamasında emek verenler gerekse Genel Kurulda emek verecek olan bütün milletvekillerine ve Meclis çalışanlarına partimiz adına teşekkürlerimizi sunuyorum.

Bu mücadelenin en önemli başlıklarından biri toplumsal cinsiyete duyarlı bütçenin olmasıdır. Ortaya koyduğumuz bu çerçeve sanılmasın ki basitçe bir ekonomik taleptir; bu yaklaşım, aynı zamanda, erkek egemen düzene politik bir müdahale ve mücadeledir. Ve sevgili kadınlar, işte, tam da bu nedenle DEM PARTİ olarak sözümüzü net söylüyoruz: Kadını görmeyen bütçe bizim bütçemiz olamaz. Her şeyden önce acilen bir kadın bakanlığı kurulmalıdır, eşit işe eşit ücret verilmelidir; güvenceli çalışma hakkını, bakım hizmetlerinin kamusal sorumluluk taşımasını, ev içi emeğin hakkının savunulmasını, sonuna kadar bunun mücadelesini yürüteceğiz. Bunun için de emeğimiz, bedenimiz, kimliğimiz üzerinde tahakküm kurmak isteyen erkek egemenliğine karşı eşitlik ve özgürlük mücadelemizi ilmek ilmek örmeye devam edeceğiz. Mücadelemiz demokratik toplumun inşasında çok önemli bir yapı taşıdır. Demokratik bir toplumsal sözleşmede temel ayaklardan biri toplumsal cinsiyete duyarlı politikalar üretmek ve aynı zamanda toplumsal cinsiyete duyarlı bir bütçenin oluşturulmasının sağlanmasıdır. Bu yüzden bizler ısrarla diyoruz ki bütçe masası aynı zamanda cinsiyet eşitlik
mücadelesinin bir masasıdır. "Kadın, yaşam, özgürlük" şiarı bütün mücadele alanlarına olduğu gibi bütçe hakkı mücadelemize de ruh vermektedir, "..."(*) (DEM PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar)
Değerli işçi, emekçi, yoksul kardeşlerim; açlık, yoksulluk kader değildir; bu sistemin ve o sistemi koruyan iktidarların yurttaşlara, sizlere, Türkiye halklarına dayatmasıdır. Üreten biz, bütçenin kaynağını oluşturan sizlersiniz, işçilerdir, emekçilerdir; asıl güç sizsiniz, yeter ki ama yeter ki bu gücü görün, bu gücü hep beraber görelim. "Ne yapabiliriz ki? Bu düzen zaten böyle gelmiş, böyle gidecek." demeyin; bu düzen böyle gitmeyecek, gitmemeli, gitmesini engellemeliyiz. Çok daha örgütlü bir mücadele yürüterek Türkiye'de işçi sınıfı başta olmak üzere emek meslek örgütleriyle birlikte ve aynı zamanda emeğin bütün alanlarında ortak bir mücadeleyle biz pekâlâ bu bütçe anlayışını da değiştirebilir, pekâlâ komple bu sistemin bütün denklemlerini altüst edebiliriz.
Türkiye'de emekçilerin direniş tarihine bakın, birkaç örnek sadece: 63 Kavel direnişi, 66 Paşabahçe grevi, 77 1 Mayısı, 89 bahar direnişleri, Zonguldak madenci yürüyüşü, TEKEL direnişi ve daha nice mücadelelere imza attık; baskıyla ve -tırnak içinde- terör yaftasıyla. Bu hafızayı silmeye kalkıyorlar. Niye silmek istiyorlar? Sömürü sistemleri ve çarkları
kendilerine göre ilerleyebilsin diye bu hafızayı silmek istiyorlar. Barışı konuştuğumuz bu süreçte bizler, tam tersi, barışa ve emeğimize daha çok sahip çıkmamız gereken bir dönemdeyiz. Bugün bir işçi, emekçi kardeşimiz "Ben açım, yoksulum, ücretim bana yetmiyor, işsizim." dediğinde hemen karşısına kolluk kuvveti çıkıyor, "Hop, sen terör suçu işliyorsun!" diyor. İşte, bizler barışı bu topraklarda inşa ederek bu ceberut iktidar anlayışlarına karşı, onların elinden bu terör parantezini gelin hep beraber alalım, gelin hep beraber emek mücadelesinin ve demokrasi mücadelesinin önünü açalım. Bu ülkede barış tesis edildikçe ölümler duracak; Kürt, Türk, ezcümle bütün yurttaşlar için emek ve demokrasi mücadelesinin kapıları ardına kadar açılacak.
Sermayenin, iktidarın bu zalim bütçesine karşı biz DEM PARTİ olarak "hayır" diyeceğiz. Sadece Mecliste olmayacağız, sadece Mecliste bu bütçeye muhalefet etmiyoruz; bizler başlattığımız kampanyamızla alanlarda, meydanlarda bu bütçeye muhalefet ediyoruz. Vekillerimiz bütçeye Mecliste müdahale ederken bizler DEM PARTİ olarak bileşenlerimizle, ittifaklarımızla, emekçilerle, yoksullarla alanlarda, meydanlarda olacağız, "Ekmek ve barış için bütçe." diyeceğiz. 12 Aralıkta Türkiye'nin dört bir yanından Ankara'ya yürüyüşümüzü başlatıyoruz, 14 Aralıkta yürüyüş kollarımız Ankara'da birleşecek ve bir kez daha "Bu bütçeye hayır! Ekmek ve barış için bütçe." diyeceğiz.
Bizler ülkemizde yaşatılan bu kara kışı bahara çevirebiliriz değerli arkadaşlar, yeter ki bu konuda bir
ortak irade sergilenebilsin ve şairin dediği gibi: "Yürüyoruz günün aydınlığında/ En zorlu iş, en ağır emek/ Ve çalışmak doğuştan mezara dek/ Ve böyle sürüp gitsin istemiyoruz/ Yaşamak için ekmek/ Ruhumuz için gül..." Herkes için barış istiyoruz.
Hepinizi saygıyla selamlıyorum. (DEM PARTİ ve CHP sıralarından alkışlar)
records.englishTranslation:
TULAY HATIMOĞULLARI ORUÇ (Adana) ON BEHALF OF DEM PARTY GROUP - Mr. President, esteemed members of parliament and the esteemed people of Türkiye who are watching us on their screens; I greet you all with love and respect, and as I begin my words, I would like to greet all our political prisoner friends, namely Figen Yüksekdağ, Selahattin Demirtaş, Leyla Güven and Ayşe Gökkan, who are unjustly and unlawfully held in prison. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
Dear friends, the Commission phase of the 2026 budget is over, there will be a busy work in the General Assembly, and a very busy work in the Commission. On behalf of my party, I would like to thank all the MPs and Parliament staff who contributed to the budget process, both at the Commission stage and at the General Assembly.
Dear people of Türkiye, the world is going through an age of uncertainty, we are literally in limbo. The order of the 20th century collapsed, but a new one could not be established. In an age of uncertainty, inequalities, climate crisis, poverty, corruption, sexism, polarization, violence are spreading everywhere. In this red apocalypse, armament is increasing intensively on a world scale. Last summer in the Hague, NATO
At its meeting, it was decided that 5 percent of the gross domestic product of each member country would be given to NATO, that is, spent on armament. A similar decision was taken in 2014, it was increased to 2 percent, but unfortunately when we look at the world, there is neither peace nor security; On the contrary, wars have increased. Peace and security are not provided by weapons, but by peace and security, freedom and a democratic order. Look, artificial intelligence, technological developments, acceleration in communication and transportation may offer humanity a wonderful living space in peace with nature, but the same obsolete capitalist system mobilizes these opportunities only to a group of capitalists, war barons, and arms dealers, and unfortunately, the positivity that should be used for humanity is used against these segments in an anti-human and anti-nature way, and again, capitalist centers are thickening their walls against the waves of migration created by this inequality. However, there are also resistance movements against these inequalities all over the world; From Seattle to Tunis to Cairo; It is a very wide network of resistance, from Gezi Park to Syntagma Square in Greece, and now it continues to grow in Nepal, Morocco, Sri Lanka, Serbia and Bulgaria as Generation Z rebellions.
We are all aware that our region is greatly affected by global wars and conflicts, trade and energy corridor wars. Russia-Ukraine war, Iran-Israel war, attempts to redesign the region by Israel and the Eastern Mediterranean.
The Cyprus problem is an issue that should be placed on the agenda of this Parliament.
Wars in the Middle East and Africa are waged with the West's high-tech weapons, but unfortunately, the financing of these weapons is provided by petrodollars in the region. The imperialist powers are literally writing a war movie and this war movie is played in the Middle East and North Africa, we are killing each other, and they are watching the movie whose script they wrote with great pleasure. We now say to them: "Stop!

We should say.
The tragedy of our region does not end. Look, people of Gaza, Gaza has been under intense occupation for more than two years. Although there is a ceasefire, we all witness that the ceasefire has not actually been implemented. We express once again from this rostrum that the Palestinian people are not alone, we will continue to stand in solidarity with the oppressed people of Palestine. (Applause from DEM PARTY and NEW YOL rows)
Dear citizens of Türkiye, it has been exactly one year since the regime change in Syria, but the waters do not calm down there. The massacres of Druze in Suwayda and Alawites in the coastal region are a great human tragedy and still continue. These pressures continue on both Christians and Sunni secular Arabs. The path to a solution in Syria is not complex or convoluted; a model in which the identities of all peoples can exist freely on the political and legal plane can well be implemented in Syria. Considering the pluralistic structure of Syria, the only solution is decentralization. In the March 10 agreement
As he found his expression; A democratic Syrian constitution written on the basis of the equal citizenship rights of Kurds, Arabs, Turkmens, Armenians, Alevis, Christians, and all different peoples and beliefs in Syria is Syria's solution and prescription. In this sense, Türkiye has great duties and responsibilities. This Parliament in Türkiye has great duties and responsibilities in this regard. We really must keep this in mind: The alliance we will establish with our Kurdish brothers on our 914-kilometer-long Syrian border and the peaceful relationship we will establish with our Kurdish brothers is the guarantee of the security of our borders. (Applause from the DEM PARTY ranks) Therefore, Türkiye's Syria strategy should fully focus here. The path to solution and democratic integration in Syria will pave the way for the establishment of a fair, equal, secular, women's libertarian and decentralized, democratic Syria.
Yes, our dear people, look, the republic is not just a form of government, but the common combination of the commons with the will to protect common and public life. If the republic is a social contract, one of the most fundamental shortcomings of this contract is that our Kurdish brothers are not included in it. The Kurdish issue is the incomplete aspect of the founding legal contract of the republic and must be completed. Türkiye should never see pluralism as a threat and danger. As people from 72 nations from different peoples and beliefs, each of us is a richness and precious part of Türkiye's geography; It should be started from here and in the 2nd century of the republic
It should stand in a place that embraces all these riches. Look, I would like to remind you of Mr. Öcalan's emphasis: "This process is the process of ensuring the participation of the Kurds in the republic through law and building the democratic republic with the broadest social unity."
As DEM PARTY, we would like to briefly summarize the minimum democratic program that will benefit this country.
The first is to ensure peace and democratic solution. This country no longer wants to bear the burden of conflict. Peace is the most humane demand of these lands, it must be realized.

The democratic solution to the Kurdish problem and the constitutional guarantee of rights and freedoms are in our common interest.
The second is a democratic republic and equal citizenship. What Türkiye needs is not conflict, polarization and discrimination, but the constitutional guarantee of equal citizenship rights.
The third is gender equality and liberal living. A budget or any policy that ignores the life rights and freedoms of women, LGBTI+ people, youth and children cannot be democratic. Gender should be the founding principle of free and democratic life.
The fourth is fair distribution and worker-oriented economy. Every budget that protects capital while workers become poor further deepens social injustice. The solution is very clear and obvious: An economy focused on labour, equality and justice, where producers have a say and decision.
Fifth is ecological life and climate justice. Türkiye's nature, soil, water and forests are being destroyed by concrete and profit-making policies. Especially
The AKP government gives away both Türkiye's assets and its budget to its supporters, and the most brutal methods are used for this. An approach that protects nature and re-establishes energy and agricultural policies based on climate justice, considering ecological balance, is mandatory.
The sixth is the democratic transformation that starts from the local level. Practices that appoint trustees to municipalities, arrest mayors and co-mayors, suppress local initiatives, ignore the local, and actually take away citizens' right to vote and be elected should be abandoned immediately. Trustee is a symbol of shame that this government has put on its forehead with its own hand in the 21st century. A social transformation that centers on local democracy, decentralization and public participation is essential. Without strengthened local governments, our cities cannot breathe, and local democracy can never be talked about. We will realize this democratic transformation ground with the idea of ​​DEM PARTY.
Yes, our dear citizens, these 6 headings are 6 items that can be the common denominator not only of the DEM PARTY but also of everyone who wants to live an equal, free, fair and honorable life in this country. This country paid a very heavy price. All of us need to establish a lasting peace in these lands, and the call made by Mr. Öcalan is a call made not only to the Kurdish people or the DEM PARTY, but to all the people of Turkey. I would especially like to underline that the PKK responded to this call, fulfilled the requirements, and compared to past experiences, the most concrete steps were taken this period.
We need to draw. Therefore, everyone here, both the state, the opposition and the government, must read this process very accurately and evaluate it very accurately. Whoever tries to instrumentalize this will lose himself and cause serious losses to Türkiye. These laws include law, law-based peace law and democratic integration laws; These laws need to be passed as soon as possible. These laws are never a matter of bargaining, these are things that must happen and must be done due to the nature of the process.

Dear citizens of Türkiye, we are talking about the budget now and while I'm making preparations, one of the most beautiful works that Aziz Nesin left us, "Yaşar Neither Yaşar nor Yaşar" comes to my mind. Yaşar, the protagonist of the novel, wants to work, he wants to get married, he wants to be a civil servant, but the state says: Wait, you're not living! But the same state issues a tax debt to Yaşar, searches for him as a military deserter, and calls him to court. Everyone in Türkiye is like Yaşar, their story is like Yaşar; When it comes to cruel taxation, this government can resurrect the dead and collect taxes, but when it comes to the service in question, "He lives, he doesn't live." he says. "None" to the citizens' demands and needs. The state that suffers knows how to take it seriously when it comes to collecting taxes from the citizens. Minimum wage workers, civil servants and retirees want a salary they can live on decently, but the state says "None." but because of the gum and bread they bought,
It collects a lot of taxes from flour and water. Students and their families ask for cleaning supplies or cleaning staff at schools, but the government says "None." But it collects tax even from the eraser used by the student. Farmers want to receive 1 percent of the national income, which is their legal right, but the government says "None." But the tax on the diesel it receives from farmers almost exceeds the price of diesel. Decree members demand rights, law and reinstatement to their duties, teachers wait for appointment, the government says "None." But he also collects taxes from the air they breathe. Small tradesmen said, "I pay so much tax, let me drink a bowl of water from the budget income." says the government, "No." He does it, but when it comes to taxes, withholding tax and SSI, he doesn't bother small tradesmen.
Meanwhile, our tradesmen in the earthquake zone - Mr. Vice President is here, I want to convey it to them personally - and taxpayers and also accountants demand that force majeure be applied again in the earthquake zone. You also know that especially our major cities; In our cities such as Hatay, Maraş and Adıyaman, the earthquake wounds are still heavy and have not been healed, and people have set up their workplaces in containers and live in containers. For this reason, I invite the Parliament to fulfill one of the most humane duties, the force majeure.
Continuing, we, as Türkiye, are a very good example of those who do not pay bills and cannot pay taxes, paying taxes. He cannot turn on his natural gas, he is cold, he cannot pay his electricity bill and these are cut off, but even though he has not paid the bill.
This is the only country that pays taxes; Such injustice, such injustice, such injustice has rarely been heard of in human history. For this reason, we underline that this budget is not a stability and welfare budget as the AKP claims, it is the budget of a handful of supporters, interest lobbies and war barons, and rest assured that today "None." At the first opportunity, you will be told "No" about what you are suffering from. will pull.
Dear citizens, valuable MPs; Yaşar Yaşarmaz's story is re-embodied today in the 2026 budget prepared by the government in Türkiye, and we all know that economic policies are not technical issues, the budget is not something consisting of cold numbers, all these are the reflection of political choices.

AKP has been bringing the same budget to the Parliament for twenty-three years, the opposition cannot get any of its proposals accepted, AKP is closed to all of them, but "We worked hard." he says. Unfortunately, they have an understanding that may not even be necessary to employ the Commission, but there is only one thing they have been consistent about throughout the twenty-three years, and we congratulate them for this stability; While they should be distributing the money to millions of citizens, they are being very consistent in giving it away to a handful of supporters. When we examine the 2026 budget proposal, we see that nearly half of it is on interest payments, defense, security expenditures, and the taxes that are not collected from them because they are used as a favor to the capital.
Look, the budget is not a solution to the problems of citizens who struggle with hunger and misery and cannot pay their bills; On the contrary, this budget makes many people living below the hunger and poverty line experience hell, and Palestinian Poet Mahmut Derviş says: "."(*) "We do not miss poverty, we miss heaven; we miss our humanity, living in a place that belongs to us." says Mahmut Derviş. (Applause from the DEM PARTY benches) Yes, humanity misses this.
Dear people of Turkey, look, today, agriculture is the lifeblood of this country, the AKP government has cut this artery and turned us from an exporting country to an importing country in agricultural products. We are one of the most suitable countries for agriculture and animal husbandry in the north, south, east and west of Türkiye, especially the Çukurova, Konya, Harran and Muş plains. The greatest evil that could be done to this country was really to end agriculture, and you did that. It is absolutely unacceptable that this country has become dependent on basic food products, and the pandemic has reminded us that industrially developed countries went hungry during the pandemic period, while agriculturally developed countries were able to feed themselves. For this reason, we say once again that this will never happen with agricultural policy, agriculture must be revived in this country. The government and capital should know this very well: To satisfy the hunger of the citizens,
We will not allow you to clothe yourself with poverty and live like Karun based on the misery of the citizens. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
Dear people of Türkiye, look, when our people hand over the steering wheel to us, our first job is to really reset and end inflation; To use the budget created by the sweat of workers, laborers and peasants for the people, not for rent and capital. Regardless of the connection or influence, we will abolish the interview and base it on merit. We will completely change the education system and make the authority of science dominant. We see the right to education, health and housing as mandatory public services and we will definitely fulfill these services. We will enact the law of political ethics and eliminate the relationship between money and politics. We will provide basic income support to our citizens. We will radically change the understanding of the government and the Ministry, which see earthquake victims as customers at TOKİ, and make Türkiye a truly earthquake-resistant country. Why shouldn't a country, nearly three quarters of which is built on a fault line, achieve what Japan has achieved?

But TOKİ's mind, the Ministry's mind is only "Let me build as many houses as I can and try to sell them to the earthquake victims at 100 percent profit." They think about it. We will provide employment for disabled people. We will definitely change all institutions and approaches that ignore the 10 million disabled people living in this country and foster discrimination. We will not pay treasury guarantees. Interest debts of tradesmen, students, retirees and workers to interest lobbies and banks
We will delete it completely. These are the first steps towards the correct functioning of socialism for an equal, free and democratic social order. We are aware that it is not easy to continue this march until the end, we are aware that it is a difficult task, but we will continue to work, concentrate and struggle together as the DEM PARTY to realize these truths that benefit 86 million people. (Applause from DEM PARTY benches)
Dear women, if Türkiye only tried to portray the situation that we women have experienced in the last year like this, it would see the worst of violence. Social inequalities, male violence, femicide, suspicious female deaths are increasing rapidly; The judiciary continues to protect men. The government's attacks on women's achievements continue. Women are exposed to mobbing at work. Female unemployment and women's home care burden are increasing. The number of women working without security in underground workshops is increasing. Women burn to death in a perfume workshop in Dilovası. Mechanisms that strengthen gender equality have been weakened. Trustee practices, co-presidency and equal representation system and women-oriented services of our municipalities are specifically targeted. They withdrew from the Istanbul Convention. Now they are doing everything they can to prevent Law No. 6284 from being implemented effectively, but we, as women, never stand up against this dark picture. for thousands of years
We are fighting the same way we fought against the male-dominated system, we will continue to do so, and when we look at this budget, there is no woman's name in this budget. Women's poverty is a political issue as well as an economic one, and its solution is possible with political determination and political struggle. One of the most important topics of this struggle is a gender-sensitive budget. Do not think that this framework we have put forward is simply an economic demand; This approach is also a political intervention and struggle against the male-dominated order. And dear women, this is exactly why we say our word clearly as DEM PARTY: A budget that does not see women cannot be our budget. First of all, a women's ministry should be established urgently, equal pay should be given for equal work; We will defend the right to secure employment, public responsibility for care services, and the right of domestic labor, and we will fight for this until the end. For this reason, we will continue to weave our struggle for equality and freedom against male domination that wants to dominate our labor, our body and our identity. Our struggle is a very important building block in the construction of a democratic society.

One of the basic pillars of a democratic social contract is to produce gender-sensitive policies and at the same time ensure the creation of a gender-sensitive budget. That's why we insistently say that the budget table should also focus on gender equality.
It is a table of struggle. The motto "Women, life, freedom" gives spirit to our struggle for budget rights, as it does to all areas of struggle. "."(*) (Applause from the DEM PARTY rows)
Dear workers, laborers and poor brothers and sisters; Hunger and poverty are not destiny; It is the imposition of this system and the governments that protect that system on the citizens, on you, on the people of Türkiye. We are the ones who produce, you are the ones who create the source of the budget, the workers, the laborers; You are the real power, as long as you see this power, let's see this power together. "What can we do? This order is already like this, it will continue like this." Don't say; This order will not continue like this, it should not go, we must prevent it from going. By carrying out a much more organized struggle, together with the labor and professional organizations in Türkiye, especially the working class, and at the same time with a common struggle in all areas of labor, we can very well change this budget understanding and turn all the equations of this entire system upside down.
Look at the history of resistance of workers in Türkiye, just a few examples: 63 Kavel resistance, 66 Paşabahçe strike, 77 May 1, 89 spring resistance, Zonguldak miners' march, TEKEL resistance and we have undertaken many other struggles; with oppression and - in quotation marks - with the label of terrorism. They are trying to erase this memory. Why do they want to delete it? Exploitation systems and gears
They want to erase this memory so that they can move forward in their own way. In this period where we talk about peace, we are in a period where, on the contrary, we need to protect peace and our labor more. Today, a worker, our laboring brother says, "I am hungry, I am poor, my wage is not enough for me, I am unemployed." When he says this, the law enforcement officer immediately confronts him and says, "Whoa, you're committing a terrorist crime!" he says. Here, let's build peace in these lands and take this terror bracket from them against this tyrannical understanding of power, let's pave the way for the labor struggle and the struggle for democracy together. As long as peace is established in this country, deaths will stop; The doors of the struggle for labor and democracy will be wide open for Kurds, Turks and all citizens.
As the DEM PARTY, we will say "no" against this cruel budget of the capital and the government. We will not only be in the Parliament, we are not just opposing this budget in the Parliament; We oppose this budget in the fields and squares with the campaign we started. While our deputies are intervening in the budget in the Parliament, we, as the DEM PARTY, will be in the fields and squares with our components, our alliances, the workers and the poor, "Budget for bread and peace." we will say. On December 12, we start our march from all over Türkiye to Ankara. On December 14, our march branches will unite in Ankara and once again say "No to this budget! Budget for bread and peace." we will say.

Dear friends, we can turn this dark winter in our country into spring, as long as we do something about it.
so that the common will can be displayed and as the poet says: "We walk in the light of the day/ The hardest work, the hardest labor/ And working from birth to the grave/ And we don't want it to go on like this/ Bread for living/ Roses for our soul." We want peace for everyone.
I greet you all with respect. (Applause from DEM PARTY and CHP benches)
